testcase.name: @stynx/tenancy barrel > exports at least one symbol
MembershipAccessCache > expires entries after the configured ttl
MembershipAccessCache > evicts the oldest entry when it exceeds capacity
MembershipAccessCache > treats entries as expired exactly at the ttl boundary
MembershipAccessCache > clears all entries and invalidates only the requested tenant
TenancyPlatformAdminGuard > allows only when the platform admin flag is enabled
TenantSystemOperationSink > skips tenant membership validation records
TenantSystemOperationSink > persists system operation records and exposes the provider binding
TenantSystemOperationSink > persists system operation records without an actor id
TenantSystemOperationSink > fails explicitly when no database provider is available
TenancyController API contract > keeps tenant administration behind the platform admin guard
TenancyController API contract > delegates tenant read and mutation operations to the service
TenancyController API contract > keeps tenant mutations outside the idempotency interceptor
TenancyService > provisions a new tenant and fans out the expected side effects
TenancyService > provisions a new tenant with the requested owner id when user lookup returns no row
TenancyService > archives and purges tenants while invalidating membership cache
TenancyService > reuses existing tenants by creating a fresh owner invitation when no pending invite exists
TenancyService > lists, gets, and updates tenants with owner-scoped database calls
TenancyService > maps null tenant state and settings to active defaults
TenancyService > updates core tenant fields without writing settings when settings inputs are absent
TenancyService > updates tenant settings when only one settings field is supplied
TenancyService > falls back to null settings fields when the current row has no settings row
TenancyService > falls back to empty settings when the current settings field is absent
TenancyService > uses owner readonly transactions for single-tenant reads
TenancyService > suspends tenants with a trimmed reason and returns active session count
TenancyService > returns zero active sessions when suspension finds no active session count row
TenancyService > uses default tenant side-effect adapters when none are provided
TenancyService > throws when a tenant cannot be found
TenancyService > reuses an existing pending invitation when it still has a membership user
TenancyService > creates a fresh owner invitation for an existing user when no pending invitation remains
TenancyService > throws when a tenant disappears after an update and when the database provider is missing
TenantContextInterceptor > skips optional paths without hitting the database
TenantContextInterceptor > runs the downstream handler with tenant context and unsubscribes cleanly
TenantContextInterceptor > runs optional paths without adding tenant context
TenantContextInterceptor > forwards downstream handler errors to the subscriber
TenantContextInterceptor > rejects missing tenant context and mismatched header/claim pairs
TenantContextInterceptor > uses cached membership decisions and resolves tenant ids from subdomains
TenantContextInterceptor > trims tenant sources, validates membership through owner readonly tx, and caches the decision
TenantContextInterceptor > trims tenant ids from stynx claims and returns optional paths without a tenant
TenantContextInterceptor > resolves tenant and actor identities from bearer claims
TenantContextInterceptor > falls back from host to hostname when resolving subdomain tenants
TenantContextInterceptor > rejects malformed tenant identifiers before membership lookup
TenantContextInterceptor > rejects inactive memberships and caches the negative membership decision
TenantContextInterceptor > rejects requests without an actor identity
TenantContextInterceptor > fails explicitly when membership validation has no database provider
tenancy utils > normalizes headers, paths, optional tenancy routes, and UUIDv7 values
tenancy utils > parses bearer tenant claims defensively
tenancy utils > treats malformed bearer tokens with missing payload parts as invalid
tenancy utils > resolves subdomain tenants with default UUIDv7 and custom patterns
tenancy utils > ignores custom matchers that produce no tenant candidate
tenancy utils > creates UUIDv7 values when entropy bytes are missing variant positions
